subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF ECF/EFF, World Bank, IDB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + Honduran official (BCH) + Honduran/Spanish press (read indirectly).
A razor-thin, contested November-2025 election and transition amid a narco-state legacy; IMF-anchored macro is the lone bright spot.

Standout drags: weak/narco-tainted rule of law, high crime, extreme climate vulnerability, and contested politics; remittance-cushioned macro stability offsets. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone on political detail.

economic.notes: IMF-anchored, remittance-cushioned macro stability on a narrow, low-growth base.

fiscal_state.notes: Falling debt and a small deficit on-track under an IMF program, remittance-cushioned but low-growth and aid-dependent. (IMF reviews 2026)

monetary_independence.notes: A crawling-peg lempira, partly dollarized, with limited central-bank autonomy. (IMF; BCH)

inflation.notes: Moderate and easing toward target. (IMF; BCH)

banking_stability.notes: Sound-ish and developing, with AML/supervision gaps. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Maquila/textiles and coffee plus very large remittances (~27-31% of GDP) - narrow and dangerously remittance-dependent. (BCH; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A narco-state legacy and captured courts, with a dangerous press environment.

rule_of_law.notes: A narco-state legacy (a former president convicted in the US), captured courts, and a very low TI standing. (TI 2025; WJP; HRW)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and patronage-ridden, with a low tax take. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Among the most dangerous for journalists, with high impunity. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A contested election and transition, against extreme inequality and gang presence.

political_stability.notes: A razor-thin, contested November-2025 election and transition with disputed legitimacy and high institutional friction. (WOLA; AS/COA)

civil_liberties.notes: 'Partly free,' with activist, journalist, and land-defender killings. (Freedom House 2025; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A transit corridor with limited routes, a US-deportation receiver. (HRW; IOM)

social_cohesion.notes: Extreme inequality, gang presence, and over half the population in poverty. (World Bank; InsightCrime)

demographics.notes: Young - a dividend only if jobs appear, else a migration strain. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A historic US partner with an open economy, against gang/narco violence.

alliance_reliability.notes: A historic US partner after a period of friction, with the relationship transition-uncertain. (State Dept; WOLA)

conflict_involvement.notes: No war; gang/narco violence (scored under crime). (ACLED)

physical_practical.notes: Extreme climate vulnerability, weak healthcare, and high crime.

climate.notes: The 1998 Mitch and Eta/Iota 2020 precedent - dry-corridor plus dual-coast plus low readiness. (Germanwatch CRI long-term top-10)

healthcare.notes: Weak and underfunded, with thin rural coverage. (World Bank; PAHO)

crime_safety.notes: A homicide rate ~25-31/100k - falling but high - with extortion and gangs. (InsightCrime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against extreme hurricane exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A Central American economy whose lone bright spot is IMF-anchored, remittance-cushioned macro stability (falling debt, easing inflation) on a narrow base dangerously dependent on remittances (~30% of GDP). The drags dominate: a narco-state legacy with a former president convicted in the US and captured courts, one of the most dangerous press environments, a razor-thin and contested November-2025 election and transition, extreme inequality and gang presence, high (if falling) homicide, and among the world's highest climate vulnerability to hurricanes and drought.
